splash_regen.title: Brew a Splash Potion of Regeneration II — upgrade path before Lingering Potions

subtitle.text: Dragon Breath is the key to the top tier of potion brewing. Every other potion is a Splash Potion. This is different.

description: CH.14 · VOID ALCHEMY
▸ Lingering Potions are the final tier of vanilla brewing.
They require Dragon Breath — the only End-exclusive brewing material.
Splash Potions burst on impact and are gone.
Lingering Potions leave a cloud that persists for 30 seconds.
Any entity in the cloud receives the potion effect.
For healing: a persistent regen zone.
For combat: targeted debuff territory.
◈ BREWING CHAIN
└ Base: Awkward Potion (Water Bottle + Nether Wart)
└ Effect ingredient: add effect modifier
(Regeneration: Ghast Tear; Strength: Blaze Powder; etc.)
└ Splash conversion: add Gunpowder → Splash Potion
└ Lingering conversion: add Dragon Breath → Lingering Potion
└ Upgrade: add Glowstone Dust → Level II effect
└ Extend: add Redstone Dust → longer duration
◈ PRIORITY LINGERING POTIONS FOR CH15+
└ Lingering Regeneration II: area healing mid-boss
(throws at feet during AOE dodge window)
└ Lingering Slowness IV: slows all entities in zone
(boss crowd-control, especially for multi-phase fights)
└ Lingering Weakness: reduces boss melee damage output
(defensive; useful against Cataclysm and Divine bosses)
└ Tipped Arrows: fill area with Lingering Potion → Arrow tips
(convert 8 arrows + 1 Lingering Potion = 8 tipped arrows)
◈ DRAGON BREATH SUPPLY MATH
└ 1 Dragon Breath = 1 Splash → 1 Lingering Potion
└ Target batch: 32+ Dragon Breath per re-summon run
└ Efficient cycle: re-summon → collect during fight → brew session
└ Alchemy setup: Brewing Stand near End portal or Ender Chest
for supply without Overworld trips
▸ The Dragon's breath is its weapon.
You are putting it in bottles.
The weapon becomes medicine.
The medicine becomes armor.
This is what alchemy has always been:
converting what kills you into what keeps you alive.
▶ Collect 8 Lingering Potions
▶ Brew a Splash Potion of Regeneration II [check]
◇ Tipped Arrows are one of the best DPS investments
in Ch15-17 boss fights.
Lingering Weakness or Poison on arrows
does not require Elytras, ISS spells, or Create automation.
It requires a Brewing Stand and Dragon Breath.
You have both. Make the arrows.
